commit.message:
Implement theme system (Light / Dark / System default)

Wires the existing Settings > Theme dropdown to actual visual changes:
- Four CoffeeSettings colour accessors (ThemePanelBg, ThemeTextColor,
  ThemeDimTextColor, ThemeOutlineColor) return palette values per theme
- SyncAllWindows() broadcasts MSG_THEME_CHANGED to all open windows
- Custom Draw() methods (ParticleGaugeView, SieveDistView, CalHistView,
  ExtractionBarView, RoastGaugeView, ThumbView) use theme colours for
  backgrounds, outlines, tick marks, and labels
- BTextView tips areas update view/text colour on MSG_THEME_CHANGED and
  in constructors so newly opened windows inherit the active theme
- Coffee-colour gradients and extraction zone colours are unchanged

Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is

Initial commit: AgentForge - Multi-Model Agent Orchestrator

A multi-model agent orchestrator that accepts tasks, generates execution plans using AI models, and orchestrates specialized agents across multiple backends.

Features:
- Multi-planner support (Claude, GPT, Gemini, Ollama, Claudel)
- DAG-based execution engine with sequential, parallel, and DAG scheduling
- CLI interface with interactive config wizard
- Web dashboard with live execution view
- Agent types: claude-code, codex, gemini-cli, ollama, shell, web-fetch
- Automatic model fallback
- Task history with full output storage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Major RL upgrade: Double DQN, Prioritized Replay, Dueling Architecture

Agent (dqn-agent.js):
- Double DQN: online net selects actions, target net evaluates them,
  preventing Q-value overestimation
- Prioritized Experience Replay: samples experiences by TD error
  magnitude so the agent learns from deaths and surprises faster,
  with importance sampling weights for bias correction
- Dueling Architecture: splits network into Value stream ("how safe
  is this state?") and Advantage stream ("how much better is this
  action?"), combining as Q = V + (A - mean(A))
- Larger network (128-128 shared, 64-64 per stream)
- Custom SubtractMeanLayer for dueling combine step

Environment (game-env.js):
- 13 rays (up from 9) x 3 depth ranges (near/mid/far) = 39 readings
  giving fine-grained spatial awareness at multiple distances
- Player velocity tracking (sense of momentum/direction)
- Immediate front danger signal
- Wall proximity feature
- Frame stacking (3 frames) so agent senses motion over time
- State size: 44 per frame x 3 frames = 132 inputs

Training UI (train.html):
- Updated explainer text describing new techniques
- PR Beta stat display
- Danger indicator in top-down view
- Velocity display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Restore UWC blue redesign to landing page

Re-applies the UWC Dilijan-inspired redesign (navy/blue/#BE1E2D palette)
that was reverted during the edurate repo incident.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Switch Windows key baking to JSON file for reliable frozen-exe loading

Python module imports (try/except) are invisible to PyInstaller's static
analyzer and can fail silently in the PYZ. Plain file I/O always works.

- config.py: read _baked_keys.json via sys._MEIPASS first, then fall back
  to the Python module, then .env (local dev)
- isbn_scanner.spec: bundle _baked_keys.json as a data file
- build.yml (Windows): write both _baked_keys.json and _baked_keys.py at
  build time; clean up both after PyInstaller finishes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: 销售订单修改RMB报价自动更新KWR和USD价格 (2026-03-07 14:30)

- 修改/api/order/update API，修改price_rmb时自动计算price_kwr和price_usd
- 汇率取自uni_daily汇率表
- 前端editField函数实现局部更新，无需刷新页面

计算逻辑:
- price_kwr = price_rmb × krw_rate (1 RMB = 218 KRW)
- price_usd = price_rmb × usd_rate (1 RMB = 0.151 USD)

Fix book list cropped on mobile

The main flex container uses items-center which vertically centers
all sections. Fine for the small quiz card, but on mobile with a long
book list it either clips the top of the list or pushes the bottom
behind the nav bar. Adding self-start and w-full to the ebook section
pins it to the top of the flex container so the full list scrolls
correctly.

commit.message:
feat: scene number detection, calibration hardening, revision markers

- Add strip_scene_numbers(): detects and removes scene number lines
  (e.g. "1", "23A", "A23") that appear left/right of sluglines in
  numbered production scripts, before calibration runs. Prevents scene
  numbers from anchoring the left-margin estimate to the wrong x0.
- Add SCENE_NUMBER_RE (^[A-Z]?\d+[A-Z]?\.?$) to handle both "23A" and
  "A23" inserted-scene formats.
- Fix calibrate(): filter out x0 buckets with count < 1% of peak before
  picking the left margin. Prevents cover-page or isolated text outliers
  from skewing column detection.
- Fix slugline detection: SCENE_RE now matches regardless of x position,
  so sluglines with slightly off margins are never merged into action.
- Add revision_marker classification: asterisks (*) in the right margin
  of revised scripts are now retained and rendered as right-aligned grey
  markers rather than stripped or misclassified as transitions.
- Strip all non-ASCII characters from console output to fix cp1252
  encoding errors on Windows terminals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve QUIC stack smashing and poll timeout stalls on CI

Move large stack arrays in sendQuicPacketsBatch() to static thread_local
to prevent ~9KB stack pressure that triggers stack protector failures
under GCC 15+ (which enables -fstack-protector-strong by default).
This fixes the AsyncSocketIo crash on ubuntu-arm64 CI.

Honor QUIC poll timeout hints in drivePoll() so retransmission timers
fire promptly instead of being delayed up to 1 second per cycle. This
fixes HTTP/3 large body transfer stalls on slow CI runners.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Initial commit: AgentBox - AI Agent Orchestrator

A native macOS app for orchestrating AI agents with configurable models using a Manager-Worker pattern.

Features:
- Multi-Model Support (configurable Manager and Worker models)
- CLI Integration (Claude Code CLI, Codex CLI, Gemini CLI, Ollama)
- Local Models support (runs Ollama models locally)
- API Support (direct API calls to Anthropic, Google Gemini, OpenAI/Codex, MiniMax)
- Dark Mode (native macOS dark theme)
- Folder-Based Workflow (drop tasks in Inbox, review plans, approve execution)
- Mission Control UI for monitoring progress

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: grid cards landing page, Edit on GitHub wiki, breadcrumbs

Landing page (docs/index.md) — generated at sync time
  generate_index_md() in generate_nav.py overwrites docs/index.md with a
  Material grid-cards layout listing every synced wiki: display name, page
  count, structured-nav flag, and a Browse button. Called from sync_wikis.py
  after mkdocs.yml generation. No-op when results is empty (preserves any
  existing static file during dry-run or error conditions).
  Requires attr_list + md_in_html extensions, both added to MKDOCS_TEMPLATE.

Edit on GitHub (hooks/edit_wiki_url.py)
  MkDocs hook that overrides page.edit_url for every wiki page to point
  directly to the corresponding GitHub wiki edit view:
      https://github.com/{org}/{slug}/wiki/{PageName}/_edit
  Top-level pages (index.md) and _assets/ get edit_url cleared.
  The org name is read from extra.gh_org in mkdocs.yml, embedded by
  generate_mkdocs_yml() when the org argument is provided.
  sync_wikis.py now passes org=org to generate_mkdocs_yml().

Breadcrumbs
  Added navigation.breadcrumbs to MKDOCS_TEMPLATE features list.
  Shows the full path (Home › Repositories › My Service › API Reference)
  above each page title. Available since mkdocs-material 9.2 (we use 9.5).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(vdg): Marengo 512d embedding pipeline — root cause fix + notebook propagation

Root cause: orchestrator excluded marengo_embedding from vdg_snapshot
(model_dump exclude) but never re-injected it before save_vdg_to_db,
so video_embedding_vector was always NULL. Additionally, converter.py
omitted marengo_status/latency from provenance dict.

Fixes:
- Re-inject marengo_embedding into vdg_data before DB save (orchestrator)
- Add marengo_status + marengo_latency_ms to converter provenance
- Upgrade Marengo skip logs from info → warning for observability
- Add propagate_embeddings_to_notebook() for Marengo 512d → notebook_library
- Add vdg_marengo_coverage_ratio + vdg_notebook_embedding_coverage metrics
- Add marengo_coverage_low alert rule (< 80% triggers warning)
- Add backfill_notebook_embeddings.py script

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.0.6: add 6 individual scalar sensors for dashboards and automations

Adds visible, graphable sensors to the integration:
- Current Power (kW) — power command for the active slot
- Projected SOC (%) — expected battery level at end of current slot
- Forecast Confidence (%) — weather-adjusted solar forecast trust
- Energy Security Score (%) — how well the plan covers the bridge point
- Estimated Export Revenue — income for this optimization cycle
- Next Action — what the next slot is planning to do

All 6 appear on the integration's Entities tab, can be added to
dashboards, used in automations, and have full HA history graphs.
Also bumps sw_version to 0.0.5 in device info.
